This pick helps with traditional conservatives." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2602, "fields": { "answer": 5118, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You improve your numbers in the South, but everywhere else people aren't really a fan of this type of language." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2603, "fields": { "answer": 5119, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Fiscal Conservatives are very happy with this move, but many think cuts are unpopular during a pandemic." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2604, "fields": { "answer": 5120, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This hits the spot with many Americans who are scared of what could happen, and will be voting for you for stability." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2605, "fields": { "answer": 5121, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This announcement causes chaos in conservative areas, with many accusing you of heresy. But you may just make it up in more socially liberal areas." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2606, "fields": { "answer": 5122, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Same-sex marriage is approved in nearly every state - this answer is getting increasingly unpopular, but you manage to rally conservatives, especially in the south." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2607, "fields": { "answer": 5123, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "The Sanders campaign actually accept this 'virtual debate' offer, and steamroll you in this debate. Maybe you should've just went in person after all? The most biting attack was when Sanders said 'People are arrested for stealing bread, while the rich live on their mega mansions.' Your polling numbers go down sharply, especially in the midwest, after this 'debate'." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2608, "fields": { "answer": 5124, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This was expected by you, and Bernie expected it too. You both attack each other on this for over an hour, and by the end no one wins. Amash seems to rise in the polls afterwards." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2609, "fields": { "answer": 5125, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "The debate goes decent for you, as you say about how this would cause chaos in America, while Bernie says for the need to fix the climate. This helps you immensely in Appalachia & the Dakotas though." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2610, "fields": { "answer": 5126, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "It was all going well for you till Bernie attacked you on domestic policy, and eventually people saw Bernie as the narrow winner." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2611, "fields": { "answer": 5127, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This, this is the broadest and most normal answer on the board. It gives you a real governing message and lets you look like your building something instead of just talking about it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2612, "fields": { "answer": 5128, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is the clean conservative answer. Fiscal types will cheer, donors will nod, and libertarians will be happy\n" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 2613, "fields": { "answer": 5129, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your response is a crowd-pleaser. It gives the country something concrete, and it lets you lean into one of the issues that made you stand out in the first place." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 35000, "fields": { "answer": 50000, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your inauguration speech is a strong opening message that reinforces your youthful image and future-oriented politics. Moderates and suburban voters especially appreciate the optimistic tone, though some populists may want a more confrontational approach, and the establishment don't seem too thrilled either with some parts." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 35002, "fields": { "answer": 50001, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your answer clearly throws a bone to Trump primary voters, and strengthens your standing with blue-collar voters in the Midwest. Some establishment Republicans are nervous about the rhetoric, but your base is energized by this speech." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 35003, "fields": { "answer": 50002, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A tougher message plays well with conservatives concerned about national security and foreign policy. It may help in traditionally Republican states, though some independents see it as overly dramatic. The social conservative elements of the speech also play well in the south." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 35004, "fields": { "answer": 50003, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This bipartisan approach improves your image with swing voters and educated suburbs, though some conservative activists feel disappointed by the softer rhetoric. It does seem quite establishment though." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 40164, "fields": { "answer": 50012, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your answer will play well with pro-life voters and the religious right. It is a little louder and a little more ideological, which is great for the base and a headache for moderates." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50004, "fields": { "answer": 50016, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your response sends shockwaves through the establishment, but hits home for a lot of Americans. Although you lose quite a bit in the polls due to seeming to 'flip-flop'." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50006, "fields": { "answer": 50013, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your answer is a very realpolitik aligned answer. It keeps the tone steady, with just enough edge to sound like a president, not an activist. Although this comes at the cost of some of your hawkish base." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50010, "fields": { "answer": 50024, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This plays well with the more policy-minded crowd. It is a little cooler emotionally, but it keeps you looking like a grown-up in a fight that usually gets very stupid, very fast. " } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50406, "fields": { "answer": 50405, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You eventually keep the house, and this helps you slightly in these states. Republicans increase their majority in the senate to 52-48, flipping MO, IN & ND, while losing TX & NV." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50408, "fields": { "answer": 50407, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You are going for the more modern lane. It keeps you conservative without sounding like you think every problem can be solved by yelling at a podium. That is a strong look in a mod like this. You are slowly narrowing the Democrats lead on Black Voters." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50553, "fields": { "answer": 50552, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Critics accuse you of abandoning allies and ignoring the potential consequences of a rapid withdrawal." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50618, "fields": { "answer": 50617, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "The response draws criticism from both parties and raises questions about America's commitment to democratic allies." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50669, "fields": { "answer": 50668, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Terrible. If Sanders is clearly surging, this makes you look asleep at the wheel." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50741, "fields": { "answer": 50740, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A disaster. This is the kind of answer that will age terribly & completely make the nation question you during times of crisis! Atleast you won some Libertarians over." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50742, "fields": { "answer": 50739, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A decent middle path. It helps you look practical and keeps the federal government from becoming the entire story." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50788, "fields": { "answer": 50787, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "That is not going to sound like a real response to a national emergency. It will look lazy, and it should." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50791, "fields": { "answer": 5051, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "They call you 'Little Marco' for a reason, Marco Roboto. Wish your campaign goodbye. We advisors quit your campaign. Maybe you could talk to President Sanders about this in 2021?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50993, "fields": { "answer": 50992, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Kander storms the Midwest, saying you betrayed the heartland, and your focus on Bernie doesn't go far with someone seen as a 'real person' campaigning in states YOUR supposed to be winning!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50995, "fields": { "answer": 50994, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Clean and direct. Keeps the attack simple." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 50997, "fields": { "answer": 50996, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Oil Groups & Pro-Israel PACS help you immensely with this, fundraising millions of dollars. You didn't get to campaign, but you got appreciation from the people who really matter." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51004, "fields": { "answer": 51000, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "In a normal time, this would be a stupid decision, but in 2020 this actually helps and let's you focus more on the swing states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51005, "fields": { "answer": 51001, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You decide to do a 2 week campaign tour across New England, and with you being a Bipartisan figure, you succeed immensely here. Good going Rubio!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51010, "fields": { "answer": 51006, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Simple but firm answer, all the main candidates disavow the violence, although RFK Jr, Amash's Veep, makes some subtle attacks on Asian-Americans, hurting their ticket slightly." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51011, "fields": { "answer": 51007, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Simple but direct - This helps slightly in the rust belt, as Anti-China sentiment is high there. Just watch your numbers in export-heavy states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51012, "fields": { "answer": 51008, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Quite an xenophobic dogwhistle of an attack, and this hurts your numbers significantly. Asian-Americans were shifting right recently, but you may as well have halted that for good." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51013, "fields": { "answer": 51009, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A bad way to deflect from the issue, this hurts overall." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51014, "fields": { "answer": 51002, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This hurts in certain populist states but helps in business-friendly states." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51020, "fields": { "answer": 51016, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "The ads work a charm, and make Castro seem a radical to these communities. Some claims that parts of the ads were 'racial dogwhistling' but you succeeded in your goal here anyway." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51021, "fields": { "answer": 51017, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A firm answer, helping you in some border states but some thought you could've went further" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51022, "fields": { "answer": 51018, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your strategy helps in suburban areas - pathways to citizenship have gotten more unpopular due to populist movements on both sides, but Castro is giving Bernie immense momentum in Texas." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51028, "fields": { "answer": 51024, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your team air ads, calling Baldwin a 'anti-growth democrat'. They seem to work ok on the coasts, but due to some of the states you aired the ads in being protectionist, it has actually made some of your base second guess voting for you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51029, "fields": { "answer": 51025, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Baldwin is motivating the Midwest levels never seen before, especially after the trade deals you have been enthusiastically supporting. You do make some ground in the Rockies however." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51030, "fields": { "answer": 51026, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You campaign in suburbs all across the Midwest, halting some of Baldwin's gains here. " } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51032, "fields": { "answer": 5067, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Sounds like a serious president. Conservatives will not love every word, but it keeps the country from feeling like it has lost its mind. Don't go too far left though or the base will get angry with you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51033, "fields": { "answer": 5068, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Probably the safest middle path. It gives you the kind of answer that can play in the suburbs without making the base think you folded." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51034, "fields": { "answer": 5041, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Brian Kemp tears apart Sanders' socialist economics, attacking his 'ruthless' tax stances. Overall a good but modest night." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51035, "fields": { "answer": 5043, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You rally the donors & fundraisers immensely with your speech, but the base isn't too livened. Expect a small boost nonetheless." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51268, "fields": { "answer": 51267, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your considering WHAT Rubio? Most people knew what the end of the sentence meant, but this helps immensely in the south, to the detriment of everywhere else. " } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51469, "fields": { "answer": 51154, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "The rallies are more low key here but you still get your message across well with the folk there, you also get Chris Sununus endorsement, helping immensely in New Hampshire." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51605, "fields": { "answer": 51604, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is the safest answer if you want the White House to sound like it knows what it is doing. It helps down-ballot Republicans stay tied to a decent economy instead of pretending the administration has been in witness protection. Eventually you underperform, losing Nevada's senate seat to Democrats, and losing Texas to the rising star Beto O Rourke, while keeping the open FL seat & ND. While the house goes democrat, Nancy Pelosi is forced to make promises to the most radical parts of her caucus to even stay leader." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51614, "fields": { "answer": 51613, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A smart answer if you want to protect vulnerable candidates in swing districts. It is less flashy, but it stops the race from becoming one giant referendum on Rubio every five minutes. Eventually you underperform, losing Nevada's senate seat to Democrats, and losing Texas to the rising star Beto O Rourke, while flipping FL & ND. While the house goes democrat, Nancy Pelosi is forced to make promises to the most radical parts of her caucus to even stay leader." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51623, "fields": { "answer": 51622, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is the classic red-meat midterm line. Not subtle, not fancy, but very effective if you want people in the base yelling \"Let’s go\" at their phones. Eventually you underperform, losing Nevada's senate seat to Democrats, while flipping FL & ND. While the house goes democrat, Nancy Pelosi is forced to make promises to the most radical parts of her caucus to even stay leader." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 51651, "fields": { "answer": 51650, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Eventually you narrowly lose the house anyway, but you kept some vulnerable incumbents barely hanging on." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52014, "fields": { "answer": 52013, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A very vague answer. People cannot connect the dots here, it seems like you are just going to leave them be though?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52143, "fields": { "answer": 52142, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Trans rights groups are horrified with your stance here, but this motivates the conservative base, and some moderates. Expect a small boost anyway" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52167, "fields": { "answer": 52166, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Even with low approvals this goes alright in the rust belt, you gain some support there but even with this your low approval still hurts greatly." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52181, "fields": { "answer": 52180, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Conservatives are happy with this, but this further cements the partisan administration you have been leading, upsetting many moderates." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52192, "fields": { "answer": 52191, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your low approval ratings make this issue further divisive, democrats dislike you and your just losing more of your base doing this, at least you get a small boost with immigrants." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52214, "fields": { "answer": 52213, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is a more moderate lane, but with your low approval rating causing a major drop in the polls, you don't see the benefits of this." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52249, "fields": { "answer": 52248, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your response is applauded by human rights groups for sticking up to the CCP and protecting Taiwan in East Asia. Your decision to recognise them also helps immensely, and states with heavy Chinese-American populations start to shift towards you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52262, "fields": { "answer": 52261, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This response is incredibly tone-deaf to what is happening in the region right now - and when you hear the news that Bejing has landed on the island of Taiwan, you feel a deep, deep sense of regret. We are managing a war now" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52266, "fields": { "answer": 52265, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "The trade war crushed farmers in the plains states, hurting your polling immensely there, but helps in the rust belt, where people are finally hearing what they wanted for decades. China eventually kowtow on the plan." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52285, "fields": { "answer": 52284, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This flip-flop hurts you domestically, but east Asia is glad to hear it, India also reluctantly accept the offer. The Partnership is formed. China kowtow on invading Taiwan." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52399, "fields": { "answer": 52398, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "China, under the might of US force, give up, and with this, Taiwan stands free once more, with many people resigning in the CCP." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52495, "fields": { "answer": 52491, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Amash manages to get every states ballot, and it seems his vote splits evenly from you and Sanders, and this only hurts your numbers, and makes some people considering Amash flip over." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52507, "fields": { "answer": 52492, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You seem to be kowtowing to Amash, but this does help take some of his support away, you do lose some moderates to Bernie, but you make up for this with libertarians." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52510, "fields": { "answer": 52493, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Amash's numbers continue to rise, but without a statement off you, his polls start stabilising." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52531, "fields": { "answer": 52494, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This response helps take some of his support away in the south, but everywhere else nobody really cares." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52599, "fields": { "answer": 52593, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This policy is very popular nationwide - but some of your conservative supporters do not support this and go to the Amash third party campaign." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52614, "fields": { "answer": 52594, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Conservatives feel fine with this, but some moderates would prefer heavier restrictions." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52617, "fields": { "answer": 52595, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A very Libertarian answer - Amash supporters will be looking at your campaign after this statement, while some centre left moderates start going to Bernie." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52632, "fields": { "answer": 52557, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your answer heavily motivates environmentalists for Bernie - and you see environmental groups protest your rallies. You were always a moderate on the issue, why change now?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52640, "fields": { "answer": 52558, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This answer hurts your numbers in Appalachia, but everywhere else people are receptive to this kind of idea, and this wouldn't hurt the economy too much long term." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52644, "fields": { "answer": 52643, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This doesn't appeal to conservatives or environmentalists, which hurts your numbers significantly." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52683, "fields": { "answer": 52680, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Hispanics are very receptive to your message - after all, you are one yourself, and your family were from Cuba. Asian-Americans also like your message, especially on free trade. You've halted Bernie's momentum somewhat." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52689, "fields": { "answer": 52681, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This strategy goes decently, women seem quite receptive to this change of message, and have been noticed to lean conservative on trans issues. You halt Bernie's momentum slightly, although it could've been better." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52703, "fields": { "answer": 52702, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This message goes nowhere. Blue-collar voters are all in for Bernie it seems this year, and he keeps gaining momentum." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52725, "fields": { "answer": 52722, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your answer is very unpopular in 2020, and you will pay for this in the polls, especially due to the fact you have had a populist turn since coming into office." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52737, "fields": { "answer": 52723, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is a widely popular proposal, no state opposes this, and you won't lose many conservatives on an issue like the minimum wage compared to cultural issues. Enjoy your boost in support!" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52740, "fields": { "answer": 52724, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is a bit too centrist to appeal to some, but $10 an hour feels like a godsend to southerners." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52751, "fields": { "answer": 52715, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You anger some Libertarians & populists, but this is an accepted position. Although there are some reasonable concerns even to you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52755, "fields": { "answer": 52716, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is a response libertarians & populists will enjoy. Although this raises serious doubts, it has actually setback some conspiracy talk by being so open about what you think." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52759, "fields": { "answer": 52717, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A moderate response to the question, which most could agree with." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52767, "fields": { "answer": 52718, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You further improve your polling with Hispanic & Asian Americans, and this helps nationwide. Although it seems Sanders has overperformed the polls significantly..." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52771, "fields": { "answer": 52719, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your campaign puts all the effort in these states, and it helps with blue collar voters. But Sanders has seemed to overperform the polls significantly." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52779, "fields": { "answer": 52720, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your message resonates surprisingly well in California & Texas, while not doing as well in Illinois & NY. Although the bigger problem for you is, Bernie has seemed to overperform the polls significantly." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52792, "fields": { "answer": 52786, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your rallies in the region go decently, and you pull some of their support away. The bad thing is, Bernie has seemed to overperform polling..." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52794, "fields": { "answer": 52793, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Attacking her right where it matters, this hurts her immensely in hawkish states, while actually boosting her in dovish states. Tulsi attacks you and stands up for her vision, saying 'We need to be focused on actual enemies, not countries thousands of miles away'." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52822, "fields": { "answer": 52796, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your betting everything on this new strategy, and it seems to be working on the coasts. People are scared of what Gabbard could do to the US worldwide, and are voting for you in droves. When Hillary Clinton was asked who she'd vote for, she didn't respond. " } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52849, "fields": { "answer": 52797, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This goes nowhere. Establishment Democrats who wouldve considered you if you stuck to your guns are now only tactically backing you, and Gabbard keeps gaining more Libertarian support for Bernie every day." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52867, "fields": { "answer": 52866, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is the safest answer if you want the White House to sound like it knows what it is doing. It helps down-ballot Republicans stay tied to a decent economy, and eventually Republicans increase their majority in the senate to 56, flipping MO, IN, ND. FL & even New Jersey, while keeping the house safely in Republican hands." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52882, "fields": { "answer": 52881, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "A smart answer if you want to protect vulnerable candidates in swing districts. It is less flashy, but it stops the race from becoming one giant referendum on you every five minutes. Eventually Republicans increase their majority in the senate to 55-45, flipping MO, IN, ND & even New Jersey while winning a close race in Nevada. The house also stays decently Republican." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52909, "fields": { "answer": 52908, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is the classic red-meat midterm line. Not subtle, not fancy, but very effective if you want people in the base yelling \"Let’s go\" at their phones. Eventually the Republicans get a 55-45 Senate Majority, flipping ND, IN, MO & FL while keeping close races in NV & AZ. The house also narrowly stays Republican." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 52963, "fields": { "answer": 52962, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You eventually keep the house, and this helps you slightly in these states. Republicans increase their majority in the senate to 54-46, flipping key states such as MO, IN, ND, while keeping close races in Nevada & Texas." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53007, "fields": { "answer": 53006, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Your 'record' has been, to be frank, terrible so far. This goes terribly, and causes you to flip 0 seats, and the Democrats elect Chuck Schumer as the new Senate Majority Leader. Democrats also flip TX, NV & AZ off you, erasing gains you were making with Hispanic voters. The house also goes democratic, with Pelosi elected speaker." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53022, "fields": { "answer": 53021, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "With your low approvals, this strategy goes decent, and Republicans manage to narrowly hold the senate, with Nikki Haley as the tiebreaker, after keeping AZ while flipping no states and losing TX & NV. The House goes democratic though, and Pelosi is the new speaker." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53067, "fields": { "answer": 53066, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is the classic red-meat midterm line. Not subtle, not fancy, but very effective if you want people in the base yelling \"Let’s go\" at their phones. Eventually Republicans flip ND, while losing NV, AZ & TX. Republicans narrowly keep the senate through Haley being the tiebreaker vote, and the House goes democratic." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53147, "fields": { "answer": 53146, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is undoubtedly a moderation of your position here - but suburbs and moderates are happy with this, to the detriment of the base." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53171, "fields": { "answer": 53170, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "The senate barely approve Thapar, 50-50, with Haley being the tiebreaking vote. The base are motivated, but moderates aren't too pleased. Thapar is more moderate than some picks which may help too." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53197, "fields": { "answer": 53196, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Amy is rejected 53-47 in the senate, with a few moderate republicans dissenting, killing her nomination. You resign yourself to appoint someone after the election." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53241, "fields": { "answer": 53240, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This is undoubtedly a moderation of your position here - but suburbs and moderates are happy with this, to the detriment of the base." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53265, "fields": { "answer": 53264, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "The senate approve Thapar, 56-44. The base are motivated, but moderates aren't too pleased. Thapar is more moderate than some picks which may help too." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53291, "fields": { "answer": 53290, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Amy is approved 52-48 in the senate, even with a few moderate republicans dissenting, she still gets nominated, and brings a unique constitutionalist view to the court." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53334, "fields": { "answer": 53333, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "McConnell refuses to even take Garland to consideration, but some republicans, along with most democrats, narrowly get Garland through, 51-49. " } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53359, "fields": { "answer": 53358, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "McConnell keeps most republicans to vote for Ho, and he joins the Supreme Court, becoming the most conservative justice in the last couple of decades." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53401, "fields": { "answer": 53397, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Maduro is eventually captured by US Forces, and flew to the US for criminal trials in New York, and is immediately sentenced to life. Venezuela is in chaos for months, with various rebel factions gaining power, and the US backed government controlling Caracas & surrounding areas. Nonetheless, you gain big with Venezuelan-Americans." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53406, "fields": { "answer": 53398, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Maduro is captured eventually, but is given a trial in New York. The new Venezuelan Government is a mix of pro-capitalism US aligned politicians, and Maduro relics. Rebels eventually fade out across much of Venezuela as democracy is brought to the country once more. You gain big with Venezuelan-Americans." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53418, "fields": { "answer": 53399, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "This approach doesn't go very well - Maduro is captured, yes, but his loyalists fight in the streets against pro-democracy activists, and all this does is turn Venezuela into a pseudo-anarchy state." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53426, "fields": { "answer": 53424, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "American troops, aircraft & submarines bombard Cuba for hours, till the people revolt themselves against the Communist government of Cuba, and eventually America occupies the island, China gets angry, Russia gets even angrier, but there's nothing they can do. Florida becomes the next Wyoming. Your numbers rise." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53450, "fields": { "answer": 53449, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "James promotes your vision while giving a more populist message, there is a few gaffes but many people feel reminded of Obama in 2004." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53470, "fields": { "answer": 53469, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Brian Kemp tears apart Sanders' socialist economics, attacking his 'ruthless' tax stances. Overall a good but modest night." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53479, "fields": { "answer": 53478, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "Tim Scott, in a calm manner talks about his faith & upbringing, saying about the 'future of the party' and that Rubio deserves another 4 years. A great speech." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 53496, "fields": { "answer": 53495, "candidate": 1001, "answer_feedback": "You rally the donors & fundraisers immensely with your speech, but the base isn't too livened. Expect a small boost nonetheless." } } ]; campaignTrail_temp.candidate_image_url = "https://imgup.uk/i/GiLvcqHb.png"; campaignTrail_temp.running_mate_image_url = "https://imgup.uk/i/cwPsTNUN.png"; campaignTrail_temp.candidate_last_name = "marcorubio"; campaignTrail_temp.running_mate_last_name = "nikkihaley"; // [JETS_ENDINGS_START] campaignTrail_temp.multiple_endings = true; const _tctEndingDefs = [ { "id": 1780318213529, "variable": 0, "operator": ">=", "amount": 400, "endingTitle": "A New Majority", "endingSubtitle": "\"Nobody saw this coming. Not like this.\"", "endingText": "For months they said you could win.\nThen they said you could win comfortably.\nThen they said you could win big.\nNobody said this.

\nAs the night dragged on, it became clear that something strange was happening. States that had been written off months ago were falling into your column.

Counties that hadn't voted Republican in years were swinging hard. Networks kept pulling analysts onto the screen hoping somebody could explain it, but nobody really could.

\nThe final map looked less like a victory and more like a political earthquake.

\nThe old rules had simply stopped working.\nBack at headquarters, the mood had changed from celebration to disbelief. Staffers were staring at television screens, refreshing results they already knew were real. Even your closest advisors looked stunned. One of them had joked a few hours earlier about breaking 350 electoral votes. Nobody was joking anymore.

\nYou walk onto the stage to a crowd that can barely hear itself over the noise.

\nFor years, people had talked about the future of the Republican Party. Tonight, that conversation is over.

\nYou are the future of the Republican Party.

\nThe opposition will spend years trying to understand what happened here. Some will call it a rejection of radicalism. Others will call it a demand for stability. Political scientists will invent new theories.

Consultants will make fortunes pretending they knew this was coming.

\nThey didn't.

\nThe truth is much simpler.

\nAt a moment when the country felt exhausted, uncertain and divided, voters looked at the choices in front of them and decided they wanted something different.

\nThey chose you.

\nThe challenges ahead are enormous. Every president says that, but for once it isn't an exaggeration.

The economy is changing.

Technology is changing.

The world is changing.

Nothing feels as permanent as it once did.

\nBut tonight those problems can wait until tomorrow.

\nTonight, America has spoken with a voice so loud that nobody can pretend they didn't hear it.

\nFor better or worse, the next chapter belongs to Marco Rubio.

\nAnd judging by this map, the country is willing to give him every chance to write it.", "endingImage": "https://imgup.uk/i/4SoiJaxf.png", "endingHideImage": false, "endingSlidesJson": "[\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 400,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"ignore\",\n \"title\": \"A New Majority\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\\\"Nobody saw this coming. Not like this.\\\"\",\n \"content\": \"For months they said you could win.\\nThen they said you could win comfortably.\\nThen they said you could win big.\\nNobody said this.

\\nAs the night dragged on, it became clear that something strange was happening. States that had been written off months ago were falling into your column.

Counties that hadn't voted Republican in years were swinging hard. Networks kept pulling analysts onto the screen hoping somebody could explain it, but nobody really could.

\\nThe final map looked less like a victory and more like a political earthquake.

\\nThe old rules had simply stopped working.\\nBack at headquarters, the mood had changed from celebration to disbelief. Staffers were staring at television screens, refreshing results they already knew were real. Even your closest advisors looked stunned. One of them had joked a few hours earlier about breaking 350 electoral votes. Nobody was joking anymore.

\\nYou walk onto the stage to a crowd that can barely hear itself over the noise.

\\nFor years, people had talked about the future of the Republican Party. Tonight, that conversation is over.

\\nYou are the future of the Republican Party.

\\nThe opposition will spend years trying to understand what happened here. Some will call it a rejection of radicalism. Others will call it a demand for stability. Political scientists will invent new theories.

Consultants will make fortunes pretending they knew this was coming.

\\nThey didn't.

\\nThe truth is much simpler.

\\nAt a moment when the country felt exhausted, uncertain and divided, voters looked at the choices in front of them and decided they wanted something different.

\\nThey chose you.

\\nThe challenges ahead are enormous. Every president says that, but for once it isn't an exaggeration.

The economy is changing.

Technology is changing.

The world is changing.

Nothing feels as permanent as it once did.

\\nBut tonight those problems can wait until tomorrow.

\\nTonight, America has spoken with a voice so loud that nobody can pretend they didn't hear it.

\\nFor better or worse, the next chapter belongs to Marco Rubio.

\\nAnd judging by this map, the country is willing to give him every chance to write it.\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/4SoiJaxf.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"Gas Gas Gas\",\n \"artist\": \"Manuel\",\n \"cover\": \"https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcRC2OJhPTevGfsTVWkrACLj5QO9cIrUkPedjBkjJx5qig&s=10\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019ea29a-3c3b-72c3-be7f-64075ea110a8\"\n }\n },\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 350,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"ignore\",\n \"title\": \"Four More Years\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\\\"The country wasn't looking for a revolution. It was looking for a reason to stay the course.\\\"\",\n \"content\": \"Winning reelection is hard.

\\nWinning reelection comfortably is even harder.

\\nYet as the results came in, it became obvious that tonight would not be another nail-biter. State after state remained in your column. Some were expected. Others weren't. By midnight, most of the major networks had already moved from discussing whether you would win to discussing how big the victory would become.

\\nThe answer, it turned out, was very big.

\\nNot a historic sweep. Not a total annihilation of the opposition. But big enough that nobody could seriously argue the country had rejected your presidency.\\nThey hadn't.

\\nIn fact, millions of voters who had been unsure four years ago had made up their minds.

\\nThey'd seen your administration.

\\nThey'd seen the alternatives.

\\nAnd they had decided they were willing to stick with you.

\\nThe commentators who spent months predicting a close race suddenly found themselves scrambling for explanations.

Some pointed to the economy.

Others pointed to your handling of crises during your first term.

A few blamed a weak opposition campaign.

They'd spend years arguing over which answer was correct.

\\nThe reality was probably all of them.

\\nNo president gets a victory like this by accident.

\\nAs the celebrations continue around you, it's hard not to think back to inauguration day. Back then, there had been endless questions. Was Rubio ready? Could he hold together the coalition that elected him? Would his presidency amount to anything more than a brief political moment?

\\nTonight, those questions feel very far away.

\\nThe doubts haven't disappeared entirely. They never do. That's politics.

\\nBut nobody can deny the result.

\\nYou didn't just survive.

\\nYou earned another four years.

\\nThe crowd roars as you step onto the stage. Supporters wave signs, campaign staff hug one another, and somewhere in the back of the room a television analyst is probably trying to explain why the race they called a toss-up ended with an electoral map that looks like this.

\\nGood luck to them.

\\nFor now, the only thing that matters is the number on the screen.

\\nA second term.

\\nA stronger mandate.

\\nAnd four more years to leave your mark on the country.

\\nHistory will decide whether America made the right choice.

\\nTonight, America has already made its choice.

\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/rrSJ4H1M.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"Counting Stars\",\n \"artist\": \"OneRepublic\",\n \"cover\": \"https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/3/37/OneRepublic_Counting_Stars_cover.png\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019ea29a-3908-732f-a107-d579c27aaa1c\"\n }\n },\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 315,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"ignore\",\n \"title\": \"The Verdict\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\\\"The election was a test. The country gave its answer.\\\"\",\n \"content\": \"For a while, it looked like it might be close.

\\nThe polls tightened. The headlines grew more dramatic by the day.

Every commentator seemed convinced the race was headed for a photo finish.

By election morning, half the country had already prepared itself for a week of recounts, legal challenges, and endless arguments over who really won.

\\nNone of that happened.

\\nNot because this was a landslide. It wasn't.

\\nBut because when the votes were counted, the result was clear enough that nobody could honestly dispute it.

\\nYou had won.

\\nThe map wasn't overwhelming, but it was convincing. States that needed to stay red stayed red. States that needed to be won were won. The coalition that carried you into office four years ago had bent in places, cracked in others, but in the end it held together.

\\nThat's what reelection campaigns are really about.

\\nNot building something new.

\\nProving what you've already built can last.

\\nYour opponents spent months arguing that the country had grown tired of your administration. That voters were ready to move on. That the promise of your first campaign had faded somewhere between governing and reality.

\\nTonight, voters had the chance to agree.\\nThey didn't.

\\nAs supporters packed into the ballroom, there was relief as much as excitement. Staffers who had spent months staring at polling spreadsheets suddenly looked like they could breathe again. Advisors who publicly claimed confidence all year finally admitted how nervous they'd been.

\\nYou'd never hear the end of it.

\\nSoon enough, attention would turn to the second term. Every victory speech comes with promises. Every second term comes with expectations. Some presidents spend four years trying to get elected. Others spend the next four trying to prove they deserved it.

\\nThat challenge begins tomorrow.

\\nTonight is simpler.

\\nThe country looked at your record, looked at the alternative, and decided it wasn't finished with you yet.

\\nFor millions of Americans, that was enough.

\\nFor Marco Rubio, it was four more years.

\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/JN4GYy2f.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"Victory\",\n \"artist\": \"Two Steps From Hell\",\n \"cover\": \"https://i.scdn.co/image/ab67616d0000b273149cf6977defe909fd7d34fd\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019ec017-b622-71cb-a844-85979ca89e12\"\n }\n },\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 270,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"ignore\",\n \"title\": \"Enough\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\\\"In the end, it wasn't close enough.\\\"\",\n \"content\": \"The last few weeks of the campaign had been miserable.

\\nEvery new poll seemed to tell a different story. One day you were comfortably ahead. The next day you were supposedly on the verge of defeat. Cable news hosts spent months acting as if the election would be decided by a handful of voters somewhere in suburban America.

\\nMaybe they were right.

\\nMaybe they weren't.

\\nTonight, it doesn't really matter.

\\nYou won.

\\nNot by an overwhelming margin.

Not by the kind of result that sends political scientists scrambling for new theories. But you won clearly enough that the outcome was never seriously in doubt once the key states began to fall into place.

\\nThe opposition had hoped the country was ready to move on.

\\nThe country disagreed.

\\nAs the final electoral votes appeared on the screen, the atmosphere inside campaign headquarters changed. Not into celebration at first, but relief. Pure relief.\\nThe pressure was gone.

\\nThe endless speculation was over.
\\nThe presidency was yours for another four years.

\\nNobody will remember the individual polls.

Nobody will remember the pundits who confidently predicted one outcome before confidently predicting the exact opposite a week later.

They never do.

\\nWhat people will remember is the result.

\\nFor all the criticism, all the attacks, all the predictions of collapse, voters had looked at your first term and decided it deserved a second.

\\nThat doesn't mean they love you.

\\nIt doesn't mean every problem has been solved.

\\nAnd it certainly doesn't mean the next four years will be easy.

\\nThe map in front of you proves that much.\\nThe country remains divided. Large parts of America voted enthusiastically against you. The coalition that brought you back to the White House is real, but it isn't invincible.

\\nYou know that.

\\nEvery president who wins reelection learns the same lesson sooner or later.

\\nVictory is permission to continue, not permission to relax.

\\nStill, those concerns can wait until tomorrow.

\\nTonight, supporters are chanting your name. Staffers are taking photos they'll keep for the rest of their lives. Somewhere in the crowd, people are already talking about what history will say about your presidency.

\\nHistory can wait.

\\nFor now, one fact is enough.

\\nThe voters had their say.

\\nAnd Marco Rubio is still President of the United States.

\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/gl4wYYdS.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"Havana\",\n \"artist\": \"Camila Cabello \",\n \"cover\": \"https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/9/98/Havana_%28featuring_Young_Thug%29_%28Official_Single_Cover%29_by_Camila_Cabello.png\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019e8a0e-0088-70d2-aa11-2207726166da\"\n }\n },\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 225,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"tie\",\n \"title\": \"Nobody Won\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\\\"The voters made their choice. Washington made the rest.\\\"\",\n \"content\": \"At 11:37 PM Eastern Time, one of the major networks made a decision that would've been unthinkable a year ago.\\nThey projected that neither candidate would reach 270 electoral votes.

\\nFor a few moments, nobody quite understood what that meant.

\\nThe maps refreshed. Analysts checked their numbers. Campaign staff on both sides stared at television screens hoping somebody had made a mistake.

\\nNobody had.

\\nMarco Rubio had won states he needed.

\\nBernie Sanders had won states he needed.

\\nAnd somewhere in the middle sat Justin Amash.

\\nFor months, both parties had laughed off his campaign as a protest movement with nowhere to go. They stopped laughing when he began pulling double digits in key states. They stopped laughing entirely when he potentially carried enough electoral votes to deny either major candidate a majority.

\\nNow the entire country was about to receive a civics lesson.

\\nThe election would be decided in the House of Representatives.

\\nThe weeks that followed were ugly.

\\nLobbying. Negotiations. Rumors. Threats. Promises.

Every politician in Washington suddenly became the most important person in America.

Cable news abandoned all pretense of covering anything else.

Markets swung wildly every time a congressman gave an interview.

\\nNobody knew what would happen.

\\nNot the public.

\\nNot the campaigns.

\\nNot even Justin Amash.

\\nEspecially not Justin Amash.

\\nThe former congressman had become the most influential third-party candidate in modern American history almost by accident. Millions of Americans had voted for him to make a point.

\\nNow he held the balance of power.

\\nEventually, after weeks of chaos, a decision was made.

\\nThe House selected its president.

\\nThe Senate selected its vice president.

\\nThe constitutional crisis everyone had spent decades discussing in textbooks had finally arrived, and somehow the republic survived it.

\\nBarely.

\\nYears from now, people will argue about who really won this election.

\\nWas it Rubio?

\\nWas it Sanders?

\\nWas it Amash?

\\nThe answer depends on who you ask.

\\nBut one thing is certain.

\\nThe American people spent months choosing a president.

\\nIn the end, they chose an argument instead.

\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/novo5qzN.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"Stuck In The Middle With You\",\n \"artist\": \"Stealers Wheel\",\n \"cover\": \"https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcSjQ88XOyon1yaQAjNXSqjji3C7V1FE1a6LGhzGbT-nSw&s=10\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019e8a0e-0035-709a-96bc-249638ccb41e\"\n }\n },\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 225,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"ignore\",\n \"title\": \"The End of the Road\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\\\"The country heard your case. It chose someone else.\\\"\",\n \"content\": \"You knew before the networks called it.

\\nNot because of any single state.

Not because of a dramatic moment.

Just a feeling.

\\nThe path was getting narrower with every update.

\\nA county underperformed here. A suburb shifted there. One state stayed stubbornly out of reach. Then another.

\\nBy midnight, the math had become difficult.

\\nBy one in the morning, it had become impossible.

\\nThe campaign headquarters didn't erupt into anger or disbelief. It was too late for that. Most people just sat quietly. Some stared at their phones. Others watched the television as analysts explained the outcome to an audience that already understood it.

\\nYou had lost.

\\nNot in a landslide.

\\nNot in a humiliation.

\\nBut clearly enough.

\\nThe states you needed most had slipped away, and with them went any chance of a second term.

\\nAcross the country, supporters tried to make sense of it. Some blamed the campaign. Some blamed the media. Some blamed circumstances beyond anyone's control. The arguments would continue for years.

\\nThey always do.

\\nThe truth is usually less satisfying.

\\nElections aren't won or lost because of one decision.

\\nThey're won and lost because millions of people make millions of decisions.

\\nAnd tonight, enough of those decisions went against you.

\\nAs your advisors gather nearby, the room feels strangely calm. Exhausted, but calm.\\nThe pressure is gone now.

\\nFor four years, every decision carried the weight of a presidency. Every speech, every crisis, every headline became part of the record voters would eventually judge.\\nTonight, the verdict arrived.

\\nNot a rejection of everything you tried to do.

\\nNot an endorsement of everything your opponent promised.

\\nJust a simple conclusion from the people who mattered most.

\\nThey wanted someone else.
\\nSoon you'll walk onto the stage and deliver the speech everyone expects. You'll congratulate the winner. You'll thank your supporters. You'll talk about democracy, service, and the future.

\\nYou'll mean most of it.

\\nWhat happens next is no longer your decision.

\\nThe presidency will belong to somebody else.

\\nHistory will decide what kind of president Marco Rubio was.

\\nThe voters have already decided he won't be the next one.

\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/XzknYmHH.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"Panini\",\n \"artist\": \"Lil Nas X\",\n \"cover\": \"https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/3/32/Panini_Remix_Cover.jpg/250px-Panini_Remix_Cover.jpg\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019ea29a-3951-7117-b0d2-f4dfe73372c7\"\n }\n },\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 150,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"ignore\",\n \"title\": \"Collapse\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\\\"It didn’t fall apart all at once. That would’ve been easier.\\\"\",\n \"content\": \"The first call came earlier than expected.
\\nNot from a rival campaign. Not from a newsroom. From a state you thought you had secured.

\\nThen another followed.

\\nThen another.

\\nAt first, nobody said the word out loud. Not in the room. Not on the phones. Not even in the campaign group chats that were suddenly going silent one by one.

\\nBut everyone knew what was happening.
\\nThis wasn’t a close race slipping away.
\\nThis was a coalition breaking.
\\nBy the time the major networks began projecting states, the story had already been written. Not in Washington, but in places you never really regained control of after the midterm cycles, the polling dips, the shifts you thought you could recover from.

\\nYou couldn’t.

\\nThe map filled in slowly, but the direction was constant.

\\nAgainst you.

\\nInside headquarters, the energy never quite turns into anger. It doesn’t even fully turn into shock. It becomes something worse: acceptance too early, before the final results are even in.

\\nPeople start packing things that aren’t officially over yet.

\\nStaffers who once talked about governing plans for a second term now speak in past tense without noticing. Advisors stop checking updates every minute. Phones stay on the table, face down.

\\nNobody needs to say it.

\\nThe numbers are already saying it.

\\nThis is not a landslide, but it is not close either. It sits in the uncomfortable middle space where campaigns go to die slowly. The kind of loss that doesn’t feel like a moment, just a process.

\\nWhen the final projection comes, it doesn’t change much. It just confirms what everyone already knew by then.

\\nYou lost too many places you needed to win.

\\nAnd won too few places you could afford to lose.

\\nThere is a speech to give later. There always is. It will be about democracy, about respect, about moving forward. It will sound practiced because it is. Every candidate learns this speech long before they ever need it.

\\nBut for now, there is only the quiet after the campaign ends.

\\nThe maps will be analyzed for months.
The decisions will be debated endlessly.
People will argue about strategy, messaging, timing, all of it.
\\nThat’s what losing campaigns become.
\\nPost-mortems.

\\nFor you, though, it’s simpler than that.
\\nThe country made a choice.

\\nAnd it didn’t choose you.

\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/gncJryWw.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"Bad Habits\",\n \"artist\": \"Ed Sheeran\",\n \"cover\": \"https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/2/2e/Ed_Sheeran_-_Bad_Habits_2.png\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019eb692-f360-706e-8cc2-bf3761c2056c\"\n }\n },\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 100,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"ignore\",\n \"title\": \"Not you. Them\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\",\n \"content\": \"A crushing defeat. Humiliation. Worse than Dole. Worse than McCain. Seduced by socialist lies, the American people chose the person who wants to turn the country into Venezuela over your sensible agenda.
The promise of a free lunch was as tempting as ever—so much so that they failed to see the price tag attached. Alas, in the end, the fault lies with you.

You failed to persuade the people; your term was so disastrous that the public was willing to turn to a far-left socialist.

There is no one to blame but you.

\\n\\nThe bad news keeps coming. The Democrats swept Congress; the Blue Wave even managed to swamp Hyde-Smith’s seat in Mississippi. Sanders will have a compliant enough Congress to push through his far-left agenda and wreck the economy. Can you even imagine what’s next? Socialized medicine? Unchecked union power? Total capitulation in foreign affairs? Perhaps even worse... No, you refuse to believe it’s over. Your political career certainly is, of course, but the American system has weathered so many trials; it won't simply give in so easily. It will take four years—maybe eight—but the American people will eventually realize that socialism is nothing but a scam and elect a conservative to fix it all... Yes, it’s only a matter of time...

\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/HUxXG9xF.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"We Are The People\",\n \"artist\": \"Empire of the Sun\",\n \"cover\": \"https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/5/5e/We_are_the_people_alternate_cover.jpg\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019ec017-b530-7391-ba8d-0b42ed117d07\"\n }\n },\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 50,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"ignore\",\n \"title\": \"Dam Collapse\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\",\n \"content\": \"They say the \\\"Pink Tide\\\" is a Latin American phenomenon. They believe it could never happen in the United States—that the American capitalist system is so robust that such absurd notions of wealth redistribution would never garner public support. Yet, as you watch a \\\"blue wave\\\" sweep across former Republican strongholds, you cannot help but realize the fallacy of that view. No, the United States was merely spared, for a time, from the populist tide that swept through Latin America; but the levees could not hold back the flood forever.

\\n\\nCornyn is gone. Sullivan is gone. Even McConnell is gone. In the House, Democrats have secured a majority unprecedented since 1974. Sanders will wield unprecedented power to enact whatever changes he desires. And you? You have become America’s Henrique Salas Römer—a loser easily defeated amidst the rise of a populist socialist.

\\n\\nAn era has come to an end. America will no longer be a beacon of freedom.

Everything your parents fled from will play out all over again right here. And this time, there is nowhere left to escape to.

\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/XiNKYrwG.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"Wait\",\n \"artist\": \"M83\",\n \"cover\": \"https://i1.sndcdn.com/artworks-5hW2JO35suZU-0-t500x500.jpg\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019ec017-b6ee-719d-8669-e2e6b410400f\"\n }\n },\n {\n \"variable\": 0,\n \"operator\": \">=\",\n \"amount\": 0,\n \"outcomeCondition\": \"ignore\",\n \"title\": \"**Wikipedia – United Socialist States of America**\",\n \"subtitle\": \"\",\n \"content\": \"The Socialist United States of America (USSA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America. It is a federal republic consisting of 50 states and a federal capital district, Washington, D.C. The 48 contiguous states border Canada to the north and Mexico to the south, with the semi-exclave of Alaska in the northwest and the archipelago of Hawaii in the Pacific Ocean. The United States also asserts sovereignty over five major island territories and various uninhabited islands in Oceania and the Caribbean……

\\n\\n……United States is a socialist state in which the role of the Democratic Socialist Party is enshrined in the Constitution. The United States is currently one of only two communist countries in the world outside of Asia. United States has an authoritarian government wherein political opposition is prohibited. Censorship is extensive, and independent journalism is repressed……

²\",\n \"image\": \"https://imgup.uk/i/1M9opxPq.png\",\n \"slideGroup\": \"main\",\n \"variableConditions\": [],\n \"variableConditionOperator\": \"AND\",\n \"answerConditionType\": \"ignore\",\n \"answerConditionAnswer\": \"\",\n \"answerConditionAnswers\": \"\",\n \"audio\": {\n \"title\": \"Somebody That I Used To Know\",\n \"artist\": \"Gotye\",\n \"cover\": \"https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/c1/Gotye_featuring_Kimbra_-_Somebody_That_I_Used_to_Know.png/250px-Gotye_featuring_Kimbra_-_Somebody_That_I_Used_to_Know.png\",\n \"url\": \"https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019e89fe-542e-737f-85aa-9f0f3068dc39\"\n }\n }\n]", "audioTitle": "Gas Gas Gas", "audioArtist": "Manuel", "audioCover": "https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcRC2OJhPTevGfsTVWkrACLj5QO9cIrUkPedjBkjJx5qig&s=10", "audioUrl": "https://audio.jukehost.co.uk/019ea29a-3c3b-72c3-be7f-64075ea110a8", "endingAccentColor": "#ffffff", "endingBackgroundColor": "#d4dde4", "endingTextColor": "#102457", "variableConditions": [], "variableConditionOperator": "AND", "outcomeCondition": "ignore", "answerConditionType": "ignore", "answerConditionAnswer": "", "answerConditionAnswers": "" } ]; const _tctOpMap = { ">": (a, b) => a > b, ">=": (a, b) => a >= b, "==": (a, b) => a == b, "<=": (a, b) => a <= b, "<": (a, b) => a < b, "!=": (a, b) => a != b }; const _tctGetEndingImageEl = () => { const imageContainer = document.querySelector(".person_image"); if (!imageContainer) return null; if (imageContainer.tagName && imageContainer.tagName.toLowerCase() === "img") { return imageContainer; } return imageContainer.querySelector("img"); }; const _tctReadVariable = (name) => { if (!name || typeof name !== "string") return undefined; if (!/^[A-Za-z_$][A-Za-z0-9_$]*$/.test(name)) return undefined; try { return eval("(typeof " + name + " !== 'undefined') ? " + name + " : undefined;"); } catch (_err) { return undefined; } }; const _tctParseConditionValue = (value) => { if (value == null || value === "") return ""; const n = Number(value); if (!Number.isNaN(n)) return n; return value; }; const _tctCheckOutcomeCondition = (entry, finalOutcome) => { const cond = String(entry?.outcomeCondition || "ignore"); if (cond === "ignore") return true; return cond === String(finalOutcome || ""); }; const _tctMatchesPrimaryCondition = (entry, quickstats, finalOutcome) => { if (!_tctCheckOutcomeCondition(entry, finalOutcome)) return false; const cond = String(entry?.outcomeCondition || "ignore"); if (cond !== "ignore") { // explicit outcome match bypasses stat threshold checks return true; } const opFn = _tctOpMap[entry?.operator] || _tctOpMap[">"]; const left = quickstats?.[Number(entry?.variable) || 0]; const right = Number(entry?.amount) || 0; return opFn(left, right); }; const _tctCheckExtraConditions = (entry, playerAnswers) => { // check variable conditions if (Array.isArray(entry.variableConditions) && entry.variableConditions.length > 0) { const operator = entry.variableConditionOperator || "AND"; const results = entry.variableConditions.map((cond) => { const left = _tctReadVariable(cond.variable); const right = _tctParseConditionValue(cond.value); const opFn = _tctOpMap[cond.comparator] || _tctOpMap["=="]; return opFn(left, right); }); if (operator === "AND") { if (!results.every((r) => r)) return false; } else if (operator === "OR") { if (!results.some((r) => r)) return false; } } const answerType = entry.answerConditionType || "ignore"; if (answerType !== "ignore") { const rawAnswers = String(entry.answerConditionAnswers || entry.answerConditionAnswer || "").trim(); const answerIds = rawAnswers .split(/[s,]+/) .filter((v) => v.length > 0) .map((v) => Number(v)) .filter((v) => Number.isFinite(v)); if (answerIds.length === 0) return false; const hasAny = Array.isArray(playerAnswers) && answerIds.some((id) => playerAnswers.includes(id)); if (answerType === "has" && !hasAny) return false; if (answerType === "not_has" && hasAny) return false; } return true; }; const playEndingSong = (title, artist, cover, url) => { if (!url) return; const PlaylistCtor = (typeof window !== "undefined" && window.Playlist) || (typeof Playlist !== "undefined" ? Playlist : null); const SongCtor = (typeof window !== "undefined" && window.Song) || (typeof Song !== "undefined" ? Song : null); const changePlaylistFn = (typeof window !== "undefined" && window.changePlaylist) || (typeof changePlaylist === "function" ? changePlaylist : null); if (PlaylistCtor && SongCtor && typeof changePlaylistFn === "function") { try { const playlist = new PlaylistCtor(); const song = new SongCtor(title || "", artist || "", cover || "", url); if (typeof playlist.addSong === "function") { playlist.addSong(song); changePlaylistFn(playlist); return; } } catch (err) { console.warn("Failed to route ending song:", err); } } // fallback for custom players: drive the shared audio element directly const audioEl = document.getElementById("audio") || document.querySelector("audio#audio, audio"); if (!audioEl) return; if (audioEl.src !== url) { audioEl.src = url; } const player = document.getElementById("player"); if (player) { const titleEl = player.querySelector("#title"); const artistEl = player.querySelector("#artist"); const coverEl = player.querySelector("#cover"); if (titleEl) titleEl.textContent = title || ""; if (artistEl) artistEl.textContent = artist || ""; if (coverEl && cover) coverEl.src = cover; } const playPromise = audioEl.play?.(); if (playPromise && typeof playPromise.catch === "function") { playPromise.catch(() => {}); } }; const styleEndingDescription = () => { const desc = document.querySelector("#final_results_description"); if (!desc) return; const e = campaignTrail_temp; const theme = e._endingTheme || {}; const bg = theme.backgroundColor || "#ffffff"; const fg = theme.textColor || "#000000"; const border = theme.accentColor || "#11299e"; Object.assign(desc.style, { textAlign: "left", width: "72%", maxHeight: "20em", minHeight: "8em", height: "auto", display: "block", margin: "0 auto", overflowY: "auto", overflowX: "hidden", whiteSpace: "normal", overflowWrap: "anywhere", wordBreak: "break-word", transform: "translateZ(0)", backgroundColor: bg, color: fg, border: "2px solid " + border, borderRadius: "4px", padding: "10px" }); desc.dataset.endingStyled = "1"; }; const applyEndingImage = (slide) => { const imageEl = _tctGetEndingImageEl(); if (!imageEl || !slide) return; if (slide.image === false) { imageEl.style.display = "none"; imageEl.removeAttribute("src"); return; } if (!imageEl.dataset.endingStyled) { const e = campaignTrail_temp; const theme = e._endingTheme || {}; const border = theme.accentColor || "#11299e"; Object.assign(imageEl.style, { border: "3px solid " + border, display: "block", width: "210px", height: "250px", objectFit: "cover" }); imageEl.dataset.endingStyled = "1"; } else { imageEl.style.display = "block"; const e = campaignTrail_temp; const theme = e._endingTheme || {}; imageEl.style.border = "3px solid " + (theme.accentColor || "#11299e"); } if (slide.image && imageEl.getAttribute("src") !== slide.image) { imageEl.src = slide.image; } }; const applyEndingButtons = () => { const e = campaignTrail_temp; const theme = e._endingTheme || {}; const imageEl = _tctGetEndingImageEl(); if (!imageEl || !imageEl.parentNode) return; let btnContainer = document.getElementById("ending_slide_buttons"); if (!btnContainer) { btnContainer = document.createElement("div"); btnContainer.id = "ending_slide_buttons"; Object.assign(btnContainer.style, { display: "flex", gap: "20px", left: "13px", top: "280px", position: "absolute" }); imageEl.parentNode.insertBefore(btnContainer, imageEl.nextSibling); } let buttonsHtml = ""; if (e.page > 0) { buttonsHtml += ''; } if (e.page < e.endingSlides.length - 1) { buttonsHtml += ''; } if (btnContainer.innerHTML !== buttonsHtml) { btnContainer.innerHTML = buttonsHtml; } const buttonBg = theme.accentColor || "#11299e"; const buttonText = "#ffffff"; btnContainer.querySelectorAll("button").forEach((btn) => { Object.assign(btn.style, { backgroundColor: buttonBg, border: "1px solid " + buttonBg, color: buttonText, padding: "4px 10px", cursor: "pointer" }); }); }; const syncEndingSlideDom = () => { const e = campaignTrail_temp; const slide = e.endingSlides?.[e.page]; if (!slide) return; styleEndingDescription(); applyEndingImage(slide); applyEndingButtons(); if (slide.audio && slide.audio.url) { const key = [slide.audio.title || "", slide.audio.artist || "", slide.audio.url || ""].join("|"); const globalKey = (typeof window !== "undefined" && window.__tctLastEndingAudioKey) || ""; const currentAudioEl = document.getElementById("audio") || document.querySelector("audio#audio, audio"); const currentSrc = currentAudioEl ? (currentAudioEl.currentSrc || currentAudioEl.src || "") : ""; const sameSrcLoaded = !!currentSrc && currentSrc.includes(slide.audio.url); if (e._endingAudioPlayedKey !== key && globalKey !== key && !sameSrcLoaded) { playEndingSong(slide.audio.title, slide.audio.artist, slide.audio.cover, slide.audio.url); } e._endingAudioPlayedKey = key; if (typeof window !== "undefined") { window.__tctLastEndingAudioKey = key; } } }; const syncEndingSlideDomDeferred = () => { requestAnimationFrame(() => { syncEndingSlideDom(); }); }; const _tctBuildSlides = (entry, quickstats, finalOutcome) => { if (entry && entry.endingSlidesJson) { try { const parsed = JSON.parse(entry.endingSlidesJson); if (Array.isArray(parsed) && parsed.length > 0) { const normalizeSlide = (slide) => ({ variable: Number(slide?.variable ?? 0), operator: slide?.operator || ">", amount: Number(slide?.amount ?? 0), outcomeCondition: slide?.outcomeCondition || "ignore", title: slide?.title || "", subtitle: slide?.subtitle || "", content: slide?.content || "", image: slide?.image, audio: slide?.audio && slide.audio.url ? { title: slide.audio.title || "", artist: slide.audio.artist || "", cover: slide.audio.cover || "", url: slide.audio.url || "" } : undefined }); const hasGroups = parsed.some((slide) => slide && slide.slideGroup); if (!hasGroups) { return parsed.map((slide) => normalizeSlide(slide)); } const groupMap = new Map(); const groupOrder = []; for (const slide of parsed) { const groupKey = slide?.slideGroup || "main"; if (!groupMap.has(groupKey)) { groupMap.set(groupKey, []); groupOrder.push(groupKey); } groupMap.get(groupKey).push(slide); } const playerAnswers = campaignTrail_temp?.player_answers || []; const selectedSlides = []; for (const groupKey of groupOrder) { const groupSlides = groupMap.get(groupKey) || []; let selected = groupSlides.find((slide) => { return _tctMatchesPrimaryCondition(slide, quickstats, finalOutcome) && _tctCheckExtraConditions(slide, playerAnswers); }); if (!selected) selected = groupSlides[0]; if (selected) selectedSlides.push(normalizeSlide(selected)); } return selectedSlides; } } catch (err) { console.warn("Invalid endingSlidesJson for ending", entry?.id, err); } } const fallbackSlide = { title: entry?.endingTitle || "", subtitle: entry?.endingSubtitle || "", content: entry?.endingText || "", image: entry?.endingHideImage ? false : (entry?.endingImage || undefined) }; if (entry?.audioUrl) { fallbackSlide.audio = { title: entry.audioTitle || "", artist: entry.audioArtist || "", cover: entry.audioCover || "", url: entry.audioUrl || "" }; } return [fallbackSlide]; }; const _tctConstructSlide = (direction = 1) => { const e = campaignTrail_temp; if (!Array.isArray(e.endingSlides) || e.endingSlides.length === 0) return ""; e.page += direction; if (e.page < 0) e.page = 0; if (e.page >= e.endingSlides.length) e.page = e.endingSlides.length - 1; const currentSlide = e.endingSlides[e.page]; if (!currentSlide) return ""; let html = ""; if (currentSlide.title) html += "

" + currentSlide.title + "

"; if (currentSlide.subtitle) html += "

" + currentSlide.subtitle + "

"; html += (currentSlide.content || "") + "

"; return html; }; endingConstructor = (direction = 1) => { const desc = document.querySelector("#final_results_description"); if (!desc) return; desc.innerHTML = _tctConstructSlide(direction); syncEndingSlideDomDeferred(); }; endingPicker = (out, totv, aa, quickstats) => { const playerAnswers = campaignTrail_temp?.player_answers || []; for (const entry of _tctEndingDefs) { let isMatch = false; try { const parsed = entry.endingSlidesJson ? JSON.parse(entry.endingSlidesJson) : []; if (Array.isArray(parsed) && parsed.length > 0) { const groupMap = new Map(); for (const slide of parsed) { const groupKey = slide?.slideGroup || "main"; if (!groupMap.has(groupKey)) { groupMap.set(groupKey, []); } groupMap.get(groupKey).push(slide); } const mainSlides = groupMap.get("main") || parsed; let validMain = mainSlides.find((slide) => { return _tctMatchesPrimaryCondition(slide, quickstats, out) && _tctCheckExtraConditions(slide, playerAnswers); }); if (validMain) isMatch = true; } else { // legacy format without json slides if (_tctMatchesPrimaryCondition(entry, quickstats, out) && _tctCheckExtraConditions(entry, playerAnswers)) { isMatch = true; } } } catch(e) { // legacy format without json slides if (_tctMatchesPrimaryCondition(entry, quickstats, out) && _tctCheckExtraConditions(entry, playerAnswers)) { isMatch = true; } } if (isMatch) { const e = campaignTrail_temp; e.multiple_endings = true; e.page = -1; e._endingTheme = { accentColor: entry?.endingAccentColor || "#11299e", backgroundColor: entry?.endingBackgroundColor || "#ffffff", textColor: entry?.endingTextColor || "#000000" }; e.endingSlides = _tctBuildSlides(entry, quickstats, out); e._endingAudioPlayedKey = (typeof window !== "undefined" && window.__tctLastEndingAudioKey) || ""; const html = _tctConstructSlide(1); syncEndingSlideDomDeferred(); return html; } } return ""; }; // [JETS_ENDINGS_END] //#startcode campaignTrail_temp.multiple_endings = true; // CYOA Variables var MidtermLoss = 0; var MidtermWin = 0; var Libertarian = 0; var Hawkish = 0; var Approval = 52; var Technocrat = 0; var Futurist = 0; var Establishment = 0; var Populist = 0; var Bipartisan = 0; var Moderate = 0; campaignTrail_temp.cyoa = true; // pk -> index lookup for questions let _questionIdxByPk = null; function _rebuildQuestionIdxMap() { _questionIdxByPk = new Map(); const arr = campaignTrail_temp.questions_json; for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) { _questionIdxByPk.set(Number(arr[i].pk), i); } } // returns the true array index (>= 0) or -1 if not found function getQuestionIndexFromPk(pk) { const n = Number(pk); if (!_questionIdxByPk) _rebuildQuestionIdxMap(); let idx = _questionIdxByPk.get(n); // if map is stale, rebuild once if (idx == null || campaignTrail_temp.questions_json[idx]?.pk !== n) { _rebuildQuestionIdxMap();
